overall_summary: Development has pivoted toward elizaOS v2.0.0 architectural decisions and multi-chain data integration, while the community is demanding immediate transparency regarding the ai16z to elizaOS token migration analytics.
key_facts: Dinesh announced the addition of 100+ onchain data support via goldrush.dev integration for the plugin-evm component.
Odilitime introduced PR #6597 for v2.0.0, implementing a new skills folder structure to the core framework.
The v2.0.0 architectural proposal suggests shipping with zero default skills to prevent the 'plugin bloat' seen in version 0.x.
Community analysis reported approximately 100,000 unique addresses still hold ai16z tokens, suggesting only a 5-10% migration rate to elizaOS.
Effect AI proposed an API integration to allow ElizaOS agents to post tasks to a decentralized human worker network.
The x402Guard security proxy was introduced to provide non-custodial spend limits and contract whitelisting for DeFi agents on Base and Solana.
OpenClaw reported sold-out Mac Mini hardware in China, driven specifically by demand for dedicated AI agent hosting.
The SAID Protocol was integrated to provide digital identities for agents on the Solana blockchain.
open_questions: What percentage of ai16z tokens actually migrated to elizaOS?
What will be done with the unmigrated elizaOS tokens originally designated for the community?
What is the actual token breakdown of elizaOS supply after migration?
What will be done with the ai16z supply that was collected during migration?
Should we ship v2.0.0 with 0 skills and promote external skill hosting/discovery?
Are there situations where ElizaOS agents hit walls because they need human intervention?
Can I use Eliza to develop Solana dApps?
What is the prediction accuracy threshold being targeted for the Milady prediction market system?

new_issues_prs.title: Runtime refactor and skills folder implementation
new_issues_prs.author: Odilitime
new_issues_prs.significance: Introduces the architectural shift for v2.0.0 skills management.

new_issues_prs.title: Database refactor for long-term architecture
new_issues_prs.author: unknown
new_issues_prs.significance: Major overhaul to improve system speed and reliability.

overall_focus.claim: Focus on clearing functional blockers for beta launch, including dashboard bug fixes and removing text limits.
Integration of decentralized identity standards through the SAID Protocol on Solana.

discord_updates.summary: Heavy focus on token migration transparency concerns and the introduction of the Z1N on-chain signaling protocol.
Discussion of human-in-the-loop (HITL) capabilities via Effect AI and technical plugin fixes for EVM chains.
Architectural debate regarding decentralized skill discovery (skills.md) versus a centralized core folder.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Users expressed significant concern over the lack of documentation on the ai16z-to-elizaOS migration completion rates.
Demand for UI trust signals, better error handling, and context persistence to make agents production-ready for enterprises.

strategic_insights.insight: Shifting skill hosting to individual domains (yourdomain.com/skills.md) reduces core maintainer burden but may fragment user experience.
implications_or_questions: How will users verify the security of externally hosted skills?

strategic_insights.insight: The 'OpenClaw' phenomenon in China demonstrates that AI agent software can act as a significant driver for local hardware sales (Mac Minis).
implications_or_questions: Should the project explore official hardware partnerships or optimized OS images?

market_analysis.observation: Migration analytics suggest a high percentage (approx. 54%) of the elizaOS supply remains in limbo due to unmigrated ai16z tokens.
market_analysis.relevance: Direct impact on token liquidity and community confidence in team transparency.

market_analysis.observation: Stock surges for MiniMax and Zhipu AI in Hong Kong following the launch of OpenClaw-compatible tools.
market_analysis.relevance: Reflects broad market recognition of the ElizaOS ecosystem reaching traditional finance sectors.
